_comment: 01-install-vscode config. The 'mimeCleanup' block is a STRICT allow-list of .desktop files written by VS Code's apt/snap shell integration. Uninstall scrubs ONLY these exact tokens from mimeapps.list / defaults.list -- never deletes whole files, never touches sibling associations.
notes: Microsoft apt repo OR snap classic

mimeCleanup._enabledComment: Set false to keep MIME associations even after uninstall (e.g. you reinstalled VS Code via flatpak and don't want those handlers wiped).
mimeCleanup._desktopEntryComment: Directories searched for VS Code .desktop files whose MimeType= / Actions= / [Desktop Action *] groups should be stripped. We ONLY touch files whose basename matches mimeCleanup.desktopFiles[] above -- every other .desktop in these dirs is left alone byte-for-byte. Keys we strip: MimeType, Actions, and any [Desktop Action <name>] group block. Keys preserved: Name, GenericName, Comment, Exec, TryExec, Icon, Type, Categories, StartupNotify, StartupWMClass, Keywords, NoDisplay, Hidden, OnlyShowIn, NotShowIn, X-*.
